channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Crosby and Rakell lead Penguins to shootout win over Jets
item.description: The Pittsburgh Penguins edged the Winnipeg Jets 5–4 in a thrilling shootout on Saturday, with Sidney Crosby and Rickard Rakell scoring the decisive goals. The victory gave Pittsburgh another valuable ...

item.title: Liz Jackson Voices Frustration over Petrol Station Fuel Limits amid Shortage Concerns
item.description: Digital influencer Liz Jackson has raised alarm about the ongoing fuel shortage after being limited to purchasing only KSh 2,000 worth of petrol at her usual filling station. The celebrity, known for ...

item.title: Ottawa’s First Romance Bookstore Opens to Celebrate the Genre and Its Readers
item.description: A new chapter has begun for book lovers in Ottawa with the opening of the city’s first bookstore devoted entirely to romance novels. The independently owned shop offers a curated selection of romantic...

item.title: Amie Provido’s Key Role in La Salle’s UAAP Season 88 Volleyball Campaign
item.description: De La Salle University’s Lady Spiker Amie Provido has become a dependable force for the team in their UAAP Season 88 women’s volleyball campaign. Under the leadership of longtime coach Ramil de Jesus,...

item.title: Nigeria’s D’Tigress maintain 8th spot in latest FIBA Women’s World Ranking after World Cup qualifiers
item.description: Nigeria’s senior women’s basketball team, D’Tigress, don show strong form as dem maintain their hold on 8th place for the FIBA Women’s World Ranking after their campaign for Group A inside the 2026 FI...

item.title: Alex Eala triumphs over Magda Linette in Miami Open
item.description: Alex Eala continued her impressive run at the 2026 Miami Open, securing another high-level victory by defeating Poland’s Magda Linette. In a thrilling match, the 19-year-old Filipino tennis star displ...

item.title: Iranian missile attack injures several in Israel near nuclear research site
item.description: An Iranian missile strike on Saturday caused dozens of injuries in Israel after projectiles targeted areas close to a nuclear research facility. According to early reports, the attack struck regions s...

item.title: Segun Odegbami blasts CAF for overturning AFCON 2025 result, calls for Senegal's reinstatement
item.description: Former Super Eagles star, Segun Odegbami, don dey vex with how the Confederation of African Football (CAF) carry handle the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final. The man talk say the decision wey ...

item.title: Budget Support for SABC Highlights Deeper Structural Funding Problems
item.description: In his opinion piece, Professor Mandla Radebe critically analyses the South African government's 2026 national budget and its implications for the SABC. Despite the budget allocating R728.8 million to...

item.title: Los Angeles County CEO Fesia Davenport to Resign April 16 Due to Health Issues
item.description: Fesia Davenport, the chief executive officer of Los Angeles County, has announced her resignation effective April 16, 2026, primarily due to hereditary health issues that surfaced late last year. She ...

item.title: Doja Cat Makes South African Debut at Global Citizen Move Afrika 2026 in Pretoria
item.description: On 20 March 2026, Pretoria’s SunBet Arena was buzzing with excitement as Doja Cat performed in South Africa for the very first time during the Global Citizen Move Afrika 2026 tour. The event celebrate...

item.title: Anne Amadi appointed as Kenya’s first female judge at the East African Court of Justice
item.description: On Saturday, March 7, 2026, Anne Amadi was sworn in as a judge of the First Instance Division of the East African Court of Justice (EACJ), becoming the first Kenyan woman to hold this position. Her ap...

item.title: APC To Host Grand Reception In Zamfara For Governor Lawal’s Return To Party
item.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima go waka enter Zamfara State on Tuesday as the All Progressives Congress (APC) don set everything ready to officially welcome Governor Dauda Lawal back into the party. Th...

item.title: How tourist destinations are reducing visitor numbers without harming local economies
item.description: After the Milan Cortina Winter Olympics and Paralympics, Italy experienced a surge in travel interest, illustrating how global events can prompt short-term tourism spikes with long-term consequences. ...

item.title: Iranian Missile Hits Arad, Israel: Dozens Injured Near Nuclear Site
item.description: An Iranian missile struck the southern Israeli city of Arad late Saturday, injuring dozens of people in what marks a significant escalation in the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict now entering its fourth ...

item.title: Multiple Serious Incidents Keep Queensland Emergency Services Busy
item.description: Queensland emergency services faced a particularly challenging night over Saturday and into Sunday, responding to multiple serious incidents. In Logan Central, police shot a man in his 30s who was arm...

item.title: Omar Abdullah questions J&K administration over restriction on Eid prayers at Jamia Mosque
item.description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah expressed serious concern on Saturday over authorities not permitting Eid prayers at Srinagar's historic Jamia Mosque. Speaking at the Hazratbal shrine, ...

item.title: Canadians Prepare for Humanitarian Flotilla to Gaza Amid Potential Detention
item.description: A group of Canadians is planning to participate in a humanitarian flotilla bound for Gaza, aiming to deliver aid and challenge the longstanding Israeli naval blockade. The Global Sumud Flotilla, in co...

item.title: Global Energy Crisis Triggered by Iran Conflict Forces Major Consumption Cuts and Price Increases
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, specifically the war between Iran and Israel, has caused a massive disruption in the global energy market. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical ship...

item.title: Trump’s claim of ‘obliterating’ Iran’s military infrastructure questioned after missile attacks
item.description: After weeks of US and Israeli airstrikes against Iran's military infrastructure, President Donald Trump declared that Iran's ballistic missile capabilities had been 'completely obliterated'. However, ...
